questions.question: What is the primary characteristic of a distributed-memory system?
options: A) All processes share a common memory space
B) Processes are connected through a network
C) All processes run on the same physical machine
questions.explanation: In a distributed-memory system, each process has its own memory space and communicates with other processes through a network.

questions.question: What is the primary method of communication in a shared-memory system?
options: C) Through a common memory space
questions.explanation: In a shared-memory system, processes share a common memory space, and communication occurs directly through this shared memory.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using shared-memory over distributed-memory systems?
questions.explanation: Shared-memory systems offer faster communication between processes, as they share the same memory space.

questions.question: How do processes in a distributed-memory system coordinate their actions?
options: C) Through a single process controller
questions.explanation: In a distributed-memory system, processes coordinate their actions through message passing, where they send and receive messages to achieve a common goal.

questions.question: What is the primary consideration when choosing between shared-memory and distributed-memory systems?
questions.explanation: When choosing between shared-memory and distributed-memory systems, the primary consideration is the available hardware resources and the requirements of the application.
